hard_rejection_rules.consequence: Automatic rejection — score for 'Quality of GHG calculation' falls below threshold
Automatic rejection — score for 'Cost efficiency ratio' is zero

hard_rejection_rules.rule: GHG methodology incorrectly applied, assumptions not credible, or calculator not filled correctly
hard_rejection_rules.consequence: Score for 'Quality of GHG calculation' below minimum threshold → rejection

hard_rejection_rules.rule: Relevant costs calculator contains substantial errors or not filled completely
hard_rejection_rules.consequence: Score for 'Quality of cost calculation' below minimum threshold → rejection

hard_rejection_rules.rule: DNSH compliance for 'climate change mitigation' not demonstrated
hard_rejection_rules.consequence: Score below threshold for minimum requirements

subcriteria.name: Innovation in relation to the state of the art
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Degree to which the project goes beyond incremental innovation on a scale from intermediate to breakthrough innovation, including scaling-up. Quality, soundness and reliability of the information provided. European level as reference point.
what_evaluators_look_for: Comparison to commercial state-of-the-art (most similar commercially available process)
Comparison to technological state-of-the-art (highest TRL demonstrated)
Reference to Innovation Fund project portfolio for overlap check
Evidence of non-incremental innovation (process, product, business model)
Justification that the proposed scale is appropriate
Citations from feasibility study for all technical claims
subcriteria.project_strategy: Position the ISO 23247 four-layer DT as world-first integration for LFP CAM powder synthesis — no analogous system exists (document gap per research report Section 10.1). Contrast with ARTISTIC (electrode, not CAM), DiBaZ (cell production), TwinHeat (generic furnace). Emphasise: (1) multi-scale physics coupling (CALPHAD+CFD+PBM+DEM) is breakthrough at TRL3→6; (2) real-time PAT-driven Bayesian updating has no precedent in LFP manufacturing; (3) closed-loop MPC for electric calcination + milling is novel at industrial scale.

criteria.name: GHG Emission Avoidance Potential
criteria.note: No overall minimum for this criterion, but subcriteria C2.3 has a hard pass/fail quality gate

subcriteria.name: Absolute GHG emission avoidance
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Relative to the sector median (proposals at median = 1 point; proportional scoring above/below). Calculated over 10 years after entry into operation using the official GHG calculator.
subcriteria.formula: Score proportional to absolute tCO₂eq/10yr vs sector median
subcriteria.calculation_note: Need: (1) FAAM's annual LFP production = 7,000 t/yr; (2) reference energy consumption for gas-fired calcination baseline; (3) Puglia grid emission factor for electric scenario
subcriteria.project_strategy: Maximise absolute avoidance by: full electric calcination and spray drying, 100% renewable electricity procurement (PPA with Eni), scrap reduction from DT (fewer wasted batches = less embodied energy per tonne output).

subcriteria.name: Relative GHG emission avoidance
subcriteria.minimum_threshold: 50% relative avoidance required (non-pilot topics) — hard rejection below this
subcriteria.project_strategy: Electric calcination with southern Italy solar grid (Puglia ~360 g CO₂/kWh average grid, dropping to ~120 g with dedicated renewable PPA). Gas-fired baseline ~600 g CO₂/kWh equivalent. Target: >75% relative avoidance to score 3.75+/5.

subcriteria.name: Quality of GHG emission avoidance calculation and minimum requirements
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Quality and credibility of GHG calculation methodology. Minimum requirements: (a) process emissions below EU ETS benchmark; (b) relative avoidance ≥50%; (c) DNSH for climate change mitigation objective; (d) GHG calculator correctly filled.
what_evaluators_look_for: GHG calculator (Excel) fully completed — all sheets including data traceability
Reference scenario clearly defined and justified
Project scenario assumptions stated and referenced
ETS benchmark comparison included
DNSH TSC for climate change mitigation addressed
Sensitivity analysis on key assumptions
Consistent values across Part A, Part B, Part C, and financial model
subcriteria.project_strategy: Hire specialist GHG methodology consultant. Use official IF calculator template. Baseline: gas-fired rotary kiln LFP calcination (800°C, 2–4 h dwell time), relevant ETS benchmark. Project scenario: electric kiln + PPA. Document all assumptions. Score target: 5/5.

criteria.name: Project Maturity
criteria.bonus_note: CLEAN-TECH-MANUFACTURING bonus: financial close ≤2 years AND entry into operation ≤4 years after grant signature → higher score if all other aspects addressed

subcriteria.name: Technical maturity
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Technical feasibility of achieving expected outputs; understanding of technology and related technical risks; quality of information. Mandatory annex: feasibility study.
what_evaluators_look_for: Block flow diagrams and process flow diagrams
Mass and energy balances
Technology readiness level (TRL) with justification
Technical risks table (likelihood × impact × mitigation)
Due diligence reports if available
Volume of final product (7,000 t/yr LFP)
subcriteria.project_strategy: Current TRL for DT components: TRL 4-5 (models validated at lab/pilot scale). Project brings to TRL 7 (system prototype in operational environment). Strong point: Eni/FIB S.p.A. industrial track record in chemical manufacturing. Feasibility study must include: detailed FEED-level description of rotary kiln, spray dryer, ball mill, and DT architecture.

subcriteria.name: Financial maturity
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Ability to reach financial close ≤48 months; credibility of business model and financial model; financing plan and sources; strategy to secure supply/offtake; quality of debt terms; understanding of financial risks.
what_evaluators_look_for: Detailed financial model (Excel with formulas) — mandatory
Business plan annex — mandatory
CAPEX/OPEX breakdown
Expected project profitability before and after IF grant (IRR, NPV, WACC)
Financing structure: equity, debt, grant
Evidence of equity commitment (Eni/FIB balance sheets)
Debt financing status (indicative terms or mandate)
Sensitivity analysis on revenue, CAPEX, LFP price, carbon price
Financial close target date (within 4 years of GA; bonus for ≤2 years)
subcriteria.project_strategy: CAPEX €250M → max grant €150M (60%). Eni backing provides credibility. Need: LFP offtake term sheet (automotive OEM or battery cell maker), OPEX estimate, revenue forecast (LFP price trajectory 2027-2040). Financial close by Q4 2027 (within 2 years) would earn bonus points.

subcriteria.name: Operational maturity
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Credibility of implementation plan; milestone detail (financial close, entry into operation, annual reporting); project management track record; permits status and plan; public acceptance; ability to enter operation in line with or faster than market standards.
what_evaluators_look_for: Gantt chart covering grant signature → construction → commissioning → 5yr monitoring
Permitting status: EIA, environmental permits, building permits — Brindisi site is brownfield (advantage)
Site ownership / access confirmation
EPC contractor strategy
O&M strategy for DT system
Project management team CVs
Quality management and HSE plan
Entry into operation target date
subcriteria.project_strategy: Brownfield Eni site in Brindisi is major advantage (existing infrastructure, no greenfield permitting). FIB S.p.A. has Italian manufacturing track record. Target: EIA in progress or completed by application date. Project timeline: GA Q1 2027 → financial close Q4 2027 → construction start Q1 2028 → commissioning Q2 2030 → entry into operation Q3 2030.

subcriteria.name: Risk management
subcriteria.note: Covered via business plan and feasibility study annexes — not separately scored but informs C3.1/C3.2/C3.3
what_evaluators_look_for: Comprehensive risk register: technology, construction, market, regulatory, financing
Contingency provisions in budget

subcriteria.name: Replicability in terms of efficiency gains and multiple environmental impacts
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Efficient use of resources; reduction and/or more efficient use of critical raw materials; circularity, recycling, recyclability; potential to address multiple environmental impacts (biodiversity, pollution); DNSH compliance for environmental objectives other than climate mitigation.
subcriteria.project_strategy: LFP has no Co/Ni (Critical Raw Materials Act relevance). DT enables: (1) reduced Li waste through scrap reduction; (2) closed-loop material tracking for EU Battery Passport; (3) water savings in washing steps. DNSH: address water, pollution, circular economy in Section 4.1. Replication: same DT architecture applicable to NMC, LMFP, solid-state CAM manufacturing.

subcriteria.name: Contribution to Europe's industrial leadership and competitiveness
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Contribution to European industrial ecosystems (clusters, European suppliers); IP retention in EU/EEA; cooperation with EU universities/research institutions; capacity-building; due diligence on supply chain; mitigating critical raw material dependencies; knowledge sharing plan outline.
what_evaluators_look_for: EU research partnerships (universities, RTOs) — TBD partners
IP strategy: what DT IP will be retained in EU
Training and capacity building during project
Supply chain due diligence (precursor sourcing — Fe₂O₃, Li₂CO₃/LiOH, H₃PO₄)
Critical raw material dependency analysis and diversification strategy
Connection to European battery value chain (BATT4EU, EBA, EIT InnoEnergy)
subcriteria.project_strategy: Partner with Italian university (e.g., Politecnico di Torino or Università di Bari) for DT model development. Link to EBA (European Battery Alliance) and EU Battery Regulation compliance. Document: 100% EU-sourced technology stack (COMSOL is EU-based; OpenFOAM is open-source). IP: DT models and PAT calibration data owned by FAAM. Score target: 8-9/10.

subcriteria.name: Knowledge sharing — Communication, dissemination and visibility
subcriteria.note: Not separately scored; contributes to Section 4 overall impression
subcriteria.project_strategy: Conference presentations at E-MRS, ISE, Euromat. Open-data publication of anonymised DT performance benchmarks. Battery Passport dataset as open standard contribution. Visibility: EU flag acknowledgement, CINEA reporting, public project website.

subcriteria.name: Cost efficiency ratio
subcriteria.formula_note: ratio = (IF grant + other public support) / absolute GHG avoidance in tCO₂eq over 10 years
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Cost-effectiveness of grant per tonne CO₂ avoided. Lower ratio = higher score. Formula: 12 – (12 × CER / 200). CER=0 → 12pts; CER=100 → 6pts; CER=200 → 0pts and rejected.

project_specific_estimate.note_on_grant: 60% of €250M CAPEX — upper bound; actual relevant costs may be lower
project_specific_estimate.note_on_ghg: Need ≥750,000 tCO₂eq over 10yr to achieve ratio ≤€200/tCO₂eq at max grant
project_specific_estimate.project_strategy: With 7,000 t/yr LFP, electric calcination vs gas baseline: need to verify energy consumption per tonne LFP (typically 8-12 MWh/t for calcination + milling). At 12 MWh/t and Δ emission factor of ~1.0 tCO₂/MWh (gas vs renewable electric), annual avoidance = 7,000 × 12 × 1.0 = ~84,000 tCO₂/yr = 840,000 over 10yr. Ratio = €150M / 840,000 = €179/tCO₂ — WITHIN threshold. Verify with actual data.

subcriteria.name: Quality of the cost calculation and minimum requirements
subcriteria.scoring_rubric: Quality and credibility of relevant costs calculation; correct methodology applied (incremental cost vs reference plant); all non-eligible costs excluded; correct formula for cost efficiency ratio.
what_evaluators_look_for: Relevant costs calculator filled completely using IF template
Methodology clearly stated (incremental cost Option 1 or reference plant Option 2)
Separation of relevant vs. non-relevant costs
Maximum grant request ≤ 60% of relevant costs
Consistency between Part B, financial model, and Part C

bonus_points.name: Net carbon removals potential
bonus_points.applicability: Not applicable — LFP manufacturing does not generate net carbon removals

bonus_points.name: SME coordinator
bonus_points.applicability: Not applicable — FAAM (FIB S.p.A. + Eni) is not an SME

bonus_points.name: Maritime sector decarbonisation
bonus_points.applicability: Not applicable — FAAM is a terrestrial battery manufacturing project

overall_scoring_summary.funding_decision: Proposals above individual thresholds funded within available budget (€1B pool). Ranked by total weighted score. Ties broken by: (1) Degree of Innovation score, (2) GHG Avoidance score, (3) submission date.

C2_ghg_avoidance.confidence: medium — depends on GHG calculator verification

C3_project_maturity.confidence: medium — brownfield site helps; permitting TBD

C5_cost_efficiency.confidence: medium — needs GHG calculator to confirm ratio ≤€200

project_target_scores.note: All individual threshold criteria must pass — no exceptions
